frozen_components: GPT-2 small backbone (used as host for all variants)
architecture.summary: The paper analyzes latent reasoning models (LRMs) like Coconut and CODI that replace explicit chain-of-thought with continuous hidden-state computation. Information flows through recurrent or looped transformer architectures where soft-token projections from latent positions are fed back as inputs. The work does not propose a new system but rather applies causal intervention methods (causal tracing, gradient-subspace intervention, Markovian trajectory analysis) to probe which directions in latent space causally influence model outputs and how those directions evolve across reasoning steps.
trained_components: Coconut latent-thought generation via curriculum-scheduled recurrence
CODI latent-thought generation via self-distillation from CoT

biblio.title: Observable Patterns Are Not Explanations: A Causal-Geometric Analysis of Latent Reasoning Models

claims.statement: Observable latent-state patterns such as BFS-like frontiers and decodable arithmetic computation also appear in controls lacking the proposed recurrence or curriculum, and do not always causally affect behavior

Matched controls (Pause-as-Thought, perturbed curriculum) reproduce or invert observable patterns (superposition, BFS-like search, scratchpad-thinking), showing they are not specific to proposed recurrence or curriculum mechanisms

Latent-thought utilization is not binary but graded, scaling with a thought's causal effect on model behavior; influence strength varies across tasks and is concentrated in low-rank, loss-sensitive directions
On graph-hopping, thought positions show near-zero recoverability under causal tracing across all models, even when thought ablation shows small thought necessity
On graph-hopping, gradient-subspace ablation leaves accuracy intact for most models, but Pause-as-Thought and Coconut show notable flip-rates at high-α values that significantly exceed random control

Weakly influential latent thoughts on graph-hopping exhibit static or near-identity dynamics across steps (identity baseline dominates); on arithmetic-reasoning, causally influential thoughts show evolving dynamics best captured by two-layer MLP transition maps

Behavior-influential gradient-subspaces maintain stable geometric structure on graph-hopping but undergo large step-to-step rotations on arithmetic-reasoning; low-rank subspace stability correlates inversely with thought utilization necessity

Decodability (logit-lens hit rate), attention patterns, and static geometric structure alone do not establish whether observable patterns causally drive model behavior; models lacking latent-reasoning training recover similar decodable patterns
